column1: # Per-cell horizontal alignment from an xlsx import is honored by the
# grid renderer — overrides the 1-2-3 default (left for labels, right
# for numbers) when the imported xlsx carries an explicit alignment.
#   B1 'center'  horizontal = center    (text: default left → centered)
#   C1 'right'   horizontal = right     (text: default left → right)
#   D1 1234      horizontal = left      (number: default right → left)
#   E1 'wrap'    horizontal = left, vertical = top, wrap_text = true
# Trimmed content survives load (sanity — ASSERT_CELL trims whitespace).
# The render-level check: each column's 9-char slot shows the content
# padded per its explicit horizontal alignment. Fragments below are
# chosen so the padding direction is unambiguous.
# A1 "left" left-aligned in width 9  → "left     " (5 trailing spaces)
# B1 "center" centered in width 9    → " center  " (1 lead, 2 trail)
# C1 "right" right-aligned in width 9→ "    right" (4 leading spaces)
# "left" sits at the A1 left edge; "c" of "center" sits one column in
# on B1's left padding — the 6 spaces between prove A1 is left and B1
# "center" ends with B1's trailing pad (2 sp) and C1's leading pad
# (4 sp); 6 spaces between "center" and "r" of "right" prove C1 is
# right-aligned (a left-aligned C1 would show "right" directly after
# "right" flush against "1234" (zero spaces) proves C1 is right-
# aligned AND D1's number is left-aligned (the default right-align
# would put "1234" at the far end of D1, yielding 5 spaces between).
# "1234" followed by D1's 5-char trailing pad, then "wrap" at E1's
# left edge — confirms D1 is left-aligned.
